other_observers: I ran into a few other birders at the location, but was birding by myself. The bird has also been reported by many other observers.
description: Initially observed the bird flying over a medium sized pond to the SE of Golden Ponds on the South side of the St Vrain River. The bird was all white with black wing tips.Then observed the Ibis shaped bird standing on concrete outcropping on the pond. The bird was accompanied by a Spotted Sandpiper for a short period of time on the concrete outcropping. The bird had long pink legs and feet with a long curved bill. Pink facial skin extending onto light pink bill that turned dark black halfway through, fading back to a light pink, worn looking tip of the bill. Bird had a crystal clear, light blue eye with a black center.After observing for 15 minutes, bird flew North across the St. Vrain River and out of sight. I refound the bird 20 minutes later, perched in a tree above a group of Double Crested Cormorants on the Southern Island of Golden Ponds No 2. The brid then moved to the north end of the island and was observed foranging along the edge of the island for about 10-15 minutes.
photographer: Photographer: Jeff Percell, Jeff Percell, Jeff Percell, Jeff Percell
